summary:
For confirmation of internal derangements after jawbone trauma, prioritize using a CT scan due to its ability to provide clear views of bones while also assessing surrounding soft tissue structures effectively compared to MRIs that excel more with soft tissues than bony details needed here.
To further clarify based directly from medical literature:

The best choice is likely a high-resolution computed tomography (HRCT) or conventional CT since they offer excellent detail across all aspects necessary—both skeletal integrity post-fracture alignment and any associated ligamentous injuries within joint spaces essential for accurate assessment during such conditions involving temporomandibular joints' internal mechanics. The combination allows thorough evaluation without missing critical diagnostic information pertinent when dealing specifically with suspected intra-articular complications arising outwards from mandible traumas where precise anatomical knowledge matters most clinically speaking regarding treatment planning options available thereafter too. A CT provides better overall structural analysis required alongside potential functional implications related thereto immediately upon initial presentation itself thus guiding appropriate management formatted_summary:
agent2 (Round 2 summarized): For confirmation of internal derangements after jawbone trauma, prioritize using a CT scan due to its ability to provide clear views of bones while also assessing surrounding soft tissue structures effectively compared to MRIs that excel more with soft tissues than bony details needed here.
To further clarify based directly from medical literature:

The best choice is likely a high-resolution computed tomography (HRCT) or conventional CT since they offer excellent detail across all aspects necessary—both skeletal integrity post-fracture alignment and any associated ligamentous injuries within joint spaces essential for accurate assessment during such conditions involving temporomandibular joints' internal mechanics. The combination allows thorough evaluation without missing critical diagnostic information pertinent when dealing specifically with suspected intra-articular complications arising outwards from mandible traumas where precise anatomical knowledge matters most clinically speaking regarding treatment planning options available thereafter too.
